enrichment_note: Each field has been annotated with four AI enrichment variables: description (semantic purpose), transformation_notes (CCH migration rules), common_source_names (aliases in other systems), and example_values (representative data). Enrichments were authored using domain knowledge of Practice Engine and CCH Axcess Practice. Validate all FK references and enum crosswalks against live source data before use in production mapping.

table_context.tblcategory: Client category classification lookup table in Practice Engine
table_context.tblclientgainloss: Tracks client acquisition (gain) and departure (loss) events
table_context.tbldepartment: Firm department/division definitions for staff and job assignment
table_context.tblengagement: Core client engagement (matter) record — the primary client table in PE
table_context.tblgrade: Staff grade/level hierarchy (e.g., Partner, Manager, Senior, Staff)
table_context.tbljob_header: Job/project header — defines discrete engagements per client per period
table_context.tbljob_serv: Service lines associated with a job
table_context.tbljob_task: Task-level detail within a job, used for workflow and scheduling
table_context.tbljob_taxreturn: Tax return metadata linked to a job (return type, year, jurisdictions)
table_context.tbljob_tmplt_header: Reusable job template headers used to create new jobs from standard patterns
table_context.tblownertype: Lookup table defining client ownership/entity types (Individual, Partnership, etc.)
table_context.tblperson: Individual person/contact records (clients, staff, third parties)
table_context.tblservices: Service code definitions used for billing and WIP categorization
table_context.tblsiccodes: Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) codes for client industry classification
table_context.tbltimechargecode: Time charge code definitions controlling how time entries are billed
table_context.tbltran_history_wip_alloc: Historical WIP allocation transaction records (archived/posted WIP)
table_context.tbltran_history_wo_header: Historical write-off header records for completed write-off batches
table_context.tbltran_history_wo_wip_alloc: Historical WIP lines associated with completed write-off transactions
table_context.tbltrandebtor: Accounts receivable (debtor) transaction records — invoices and payments
table_context.tbltrandebtorclear: Debtor clearance records — links payments to invoices (AR application)
table_context.tbltrantypes: Transaction type lookup — defines valid WIP, AR, and billing transaction categories
table_context.tbstaff: Staff member records including billing rates, roles, and system preferences

fields.description: Surrogate primary key for the client category record.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer PK. Do not migrate directly; used as FK reference in source. Map to CCH category code via lookup.

fields.description: High-level category grouping type (e.g., 'Client', 'Industry', 'Service') used to partition category records.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH client category group. Validate against Ascend's CCH category taxonomy before loading.

fields.description: Short category code or abbreviation used to tag clients for reporting and segmentation.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Strip whitespace. Truncate to CCH category code max length (typically 10 chars).

fields.description: Full descriptive name of the client category for display in reports and filters.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Direct map to CCH category description. Max ~50 chars; truncate if necessary.

fields.description: Display sort order for category listings in the PE UI.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer. Used for UI ordering only; may not map to CCH. Include if CCH supports custom sort.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether the category is currently active and available for assignment.
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean. True/False → CCH active flag (1/0). Inactive categories should be excluded from migration unless historical records reference them.

fields.description: Foreign key linking this gain/loss event to the client record in tblengagement (ContIndex/ClientRef).
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblengagement.ContIndex. Resolve to CCH CLIENT_ID before loading. Validate referential integrity.

fields.description: Indicates the direction of the event — whether the firm gained or lost this client.
fields.transformation_notes: String enum. Expected values: 'Gain', 'Loss'. Map to CCH custom field or notes if no native equivalent.

fields.description: Calendar date on which the client gain or loss event occurred or was recorded.
fields.transformation_notes: DATE. Reformat to MM/DD/YYYY for CCH. Validate not future-dated and not before firm founding year.

fields.description: The channel or origin through which the client was acquired, or the reason for departure.
fields.transformation_notes: String free text or enum. Map to CCH referral source field where available. Standardize casing.

fields.description: Internal numeric identifier of the client involved in this gain/loss event (may duplicate ContIndex).
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK. May be the same reference as ContIndex; verify in source data. Resolve to CCH CLIENT_ID.

fields.description: Short reason code or category describing why the client was gained or lost.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH custom field or notes. Normalize to a controlled vocabulary if migrating.

fields.description: Free-text narrative providing additional context about the gain or loss event.
fields.transformation_notes: String (memo). Map to CCH client notes or custom memo field. Preserve line breaks if possible.

fields.description: Comma-separated list or description of accounts or services transferred with this gain/loss event.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Store in CCH client notes. May require parsing if structured; handle as free text if not.

fields.description: Estimated or actual annual fee revenue associated with this client gain or loss event.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG (likely stored as whole dollars or cents). Divide by 100 if cents-encoded. Map to CCH fee budget or notes.

fields.description: Special fee, bonus, or non-standard revenue component associated with the gain/loss event.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG. Same encoding as GainLossFees. Store in CCH notes if no target field available.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether this client gain was the result of a referral from another party.
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean (True/False). Map to CCH referral flag or custom field.

fields.description: Foreign key to the person or entity that made the referral, if applicable.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblperson or tblengagement. Resolve name for CCH notes field. May be None for non-referral gains.

fields.description: Timestamp of the last update to this gain/loss record, used for audit tracking.
fields.transformation_notes: DATE. Reformat to MM/DD/YYYY. Used for data lineage; not typically imported into CCH.

fields.description: Surrogate primary key for the contact relationship record.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG PK. Do not migrate directly; used as FK anchor. CCH manages its own relationship keys.

fields.description: Foreign key to the first (primary) contact in the relationship pair, referencing tblperson or tblengagement.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblperson.ContIndex. Resolve to CCH CONTACT_ID before loading.

fields.description: Foreign key to the second (related) contact in the relationship pair.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblperson.ContIndex. Resolve to CCH CONTACT_ID. Check for orphan references.

fields.description: Numeric code identifying the type of relationship between the two contacts (e.g., spouse, parent, employee).
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → relationship type lookup. Resolve code to description for CCH notes. Build value crosswalk.

fields.description: Text label or title describing the relationship (e.g., 'Spouse', 'Parent Company', 'Partner').
fields.transformation_notes: String. Direct map to CCH relationship description. Standardize casing.

fields.description: Free-text notes providing additional context about this contact relationship.
fields.transformation_notes: String (memo). Map to CCH relationship notes or client notes. Preserve for audit.

fields.description: Reference or document number associated with this relationship (e.g., POA document number).
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH document reference if applicable. Store in notes otherwise.

fields.description: Date the relationship was established or became effective.
fields.transformation_notes: DATE. Reformat to MM/DD/YYYY. Use as relationship start date in CCH.

fields.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this relationship is currently active.
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean. True → include in migration; False → exclude or flag as historical. Default: import only True records.

fields.description: Date or reason the relationship was terminated, if no longer current.
fields.transformation_notes: String (may contain date or text). If date-parseable, convert to MM/DD/YYYY. Log as historical note in CCH.

fields.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this relationship record.
fields.transformation_notes: DATE. For audit trail only; reformat to MM/DD/YYYY. Not typically loaded into CCH.

fields.description: Alphanumeric department code used as the unique identifier for this department.
fields.transformation_notes: String PK. Map to CCH office or department code. Validate uniqueness. Trim whitespace.

fields.description: Full name of the department or practice area.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH department/office name. Max ~50 chars. Standardize casing.

fields.description: Foreign key to the staff member who leads this department.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tbstaff. Resolve to CCH STAFF_ID for department head assignment.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether time charged to this department is recoverable (billable) to clients.
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean. True = recoverable/billable; False = overhead/internal. Map to CCH billable flag.

fields.description: Category code grouping this department within a broader reporting hierarchy.
fields.transformation_notes: String FK → tblcategory. Resolve description for CCH if no direct mapping exists.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether this department is currently active and available for job and staff assignment.
fields.transformation_notes: Boolean. Migrate only Active=True records unless historical data requires inactive departments.

fields.description: Surrogate primary key for the client engagement record — the core client identifier in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG PK. Map to CCH CLIENT.CLIENT_ID. All child records (jobs, WIP, debtors) link via this key. Critical for referential integrity.

fields.description: Numeric internal reference for the client, sometimes used as an alternate key alongside ClientCode.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG. May duplicate ContIndex or be a legacy number. Use ClientCode as the primary business key for CCH.

fields.description: Alphanumeric client code — the human-facing unique identifier assigned by the firm.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH CLIENT_ID. Validate: alphanumeric, no spaces, max 12 chars. Pad or reformat as required by Ascend's CCH convention.

fields.description: Full legal name of the client entity or individual as recorded in the practice management system.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH CLIENT.DISPLAY_NAME or LAST_NAME/FIRST_NAME depending on entity type. Preserve special characters. Max 100 chars.

fields.description: Abbreviated short-form code for the client, used in reports and quick lookup.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH alternate client code or search field. Max ~10 chars. Trim whitespace.

fields.description: Foreign key to the organization/entity type classification (references tblownertype).
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblownertype. Resolve to CCH entity type code (Individual, Partnership, Corp, etc.).

fields.description: Office code indicating which firm office is responsible for this client.
fields.transformation_notes: String FK → tbldepartment or office table. Map to CCH OFFICE_ID. Build crosswalk if office codes differ.

fields.description: Foreign key to the responsible partner assigned to this client engagement.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tbstaff. Resolve to CCH STAFF_ID. Map to CCH CLIENT_STAFFING with role = 'Partner'.

fields.description: Foreign key to the manager assigned to oversee this client's work.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tbstaff. Resolve to CCH STAFF_ID. Map to CCH CLIENT_STAFFING with role = 'Manager'.

fields.description: Current status of the client relationship (e.g., Active, Inactive, Prospect, Terminated).
fields.transformation_notes: String enum. Map to CCH client status field. Typical values: 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Former'. Migrate inactive clients with a flag.

fields.description: Foreign key to the staff member who last updated the client status.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tbstaff. Log as audit metadata; not typically loaded into CCH target.

fields.description: Ownership structure or equity arrangement description for the client entity.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Free text or coded value. Map to CCH notes or custom field. Useful for partnership and trust clients.

fields.description: State or jurisdiction where the client is legally domiciled for tax purposes.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG or STRING (may be FK to state lookup). Resolve to 2-char state code for CCH. Default to WA for Sweeney Conrad clients if None.

fields.description: Flag or code indicating the client is on billing hold, do-not-contact, or workflow hold.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG (0/1 or hold type code). Map to CCH client hold flag. Clients on hold should be reviewed before migration.

fields.description: Foreign key to the client who receives consolidated invoices when billing is directed to a different entity.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblengagement.ContIndex (self-referential). Null if client bills itself. Map to CCH billing client relationship.

fields.description: The fiscal year-end date for this client entity, used for tax and compliance scheduling.
fields.transformation_notes: DATE. Reformat to MM/DD/YYYY. CCH uses month/day only (e.g., '12/31'). Extract month and day; discard year component.

fields.description: Free-text memo field for general notes about the client relationship, billing arrangements, or special instructions.
fields.transformation_notes: String (memo/blob). Map to CCH CLIENT.NOTES or general remarks. Preserve line breaks. Flag for review if exceeds CCH field limit.

fields.description: Threshold type that triggers a WIP alert or billing notification for this client.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG enum (0 = None, 1 = Amount, 2 = Percentage). Map to CCH WIP settings if available.

fields.description: Maximum WIP amount allowed for this client before a billing trigger is activated.
fields.transformation_notes: DOUBLE (USD). Store as decimal. Map to CCH WIP limit field. Validate ≥ 0.

fields.description: Maximum outstanding AR balance allowed for this client before credit alerts are triggered.
fields.transformation_notes: DOUBLE (USD). Store as decimal. Map to CCH credit limit field. Validate ≥ 0.

fields.description: Foreign key to the payment terms code defining when invoices are due for this client.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → payment terms lookup. Resolve to CCH TERMS code (e.g., Net 30, Net 60). Build crosswalk.

fields.description: Billing frequency code indicating how often this client is invoiced (Monthly, Quarterly, Annually).
fields.transformation_notes: LONG enum. Map to CCH billing cycle: 1=Monthly, 2=Quarterly, 3=Annually. Confirm enum with source data values.

fields.description: Credit risk status code classifying the client's payment behavior and creditworthiness.
fields.transformation_notes: String enum. Values typically: 'Good', 'Watch', 'Stop', 'Hold'. Map to CCH credit status field or notes.

fields.description: Foreign key to the staff member responsible for managing this client's credit/collections.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tbstaff. Resolve to CCH STAFF_ID. Map to collections contact in CCH.

fields.description: Free-text notes about the client's credit history, disputes, or collection arrangements.
fields.transformation_notes: String (memo). Map to CCH client notes. Prefix with 'CREDIT NOTE:' to distinguish from general notes.
example_values: Do not send to collections without partner approval

fields.description: Foreign key to the primary collections contact person for this client.
fields.transformation_notes: LONG FK → tblperson or tblengagement. Resolve to CCH CONTACT_ID for AR contact.

fields.description: Phone number for the client's billing/credit department contact.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Reformat to (XXX) XXX-XXXX or CCH standard. Strip non-numeric characters except +, -, (, ).

fields.description: Fax number for the client's billing or credit contact (legacy field, may be empty).
fields.transformation_notes: String. Reformat as phone. Many records will be None. Migrate if populated; otherwise omit.

fields.description: Name of the individual at the client who handles billing inquiries and payments.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH billing contact name. May duplicate tblperson record; check for consistency.

fields.description: Salutation used when addressing the billing contact in correspondence.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH contact salutation field. Standard values: 'Mr.', 'Ms.', 'Dr.', 'Dear Accounts Payable'.

fields.description: Email domain or corporate domain for the client's billing department.
fields.transformation_notes: String. May be used for email validation or routing. Not a standard CCH field; migrate to notes.

fields.description: Flag or mode indicating whether this staff member uses timesheets for time entry.

fields.description: Default work start time for this staff member (used in timesheet calculations).

fields.description: Standard daily hours this staff member is expected to work.

fields.description: Flag indicating this staff member has multiple billing rates configured.

fields.description: Staff email address (duplicate or alternate field — may differ from StaffEmail).

fields.description: Target billable hours or percentage for this staff member.
fields.transformation_notes: String or DOUBLE. May be hours/year or utilization percentage. Map to CCH staff target.

fields.description: Flag or code indicating whether this staff member charges non-client time.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether time entries are auto-posted for this staff member.

fields.description: Surrogate primary key for the contact record in Practice Engine. Uniquely identifies each contact across the tblcontants table.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer PK. Do not migrate as a value; used as FK reference from tblengagement and other PE tables. Drop after join resolution.

fields.description: Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the contact within the firm's Practice Engine system. Often mirrors or relates to the client code.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Contact Code' or 'ExternalID' field if available. Validate uniqueness before load. May be used to cross-reference with tblengagement.EngContact.

fields.description: Integer lookup code indicating the type of contact (e.g., individual, company, director, partner). Resolves against a PE reference table.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK to a PE contact type lookup. Decode before migration. Map to CCH Contact Type field. Common values: 1=Individual, 2=Company. Verify lookup table with Tim.

fields.description: Full display name of the contact. May hold either a personal full name or a company name depending on ContType.
fields.transformation_notes: String. For individual contacts, consider splitting into first/last if CCH target requires separate name fields. For company-type contacts, map directly to CCH 'Company Name'. Trim whitespace.

fields.description: Abbreviated or short version of the contact name, used for display in lists and reports within Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Short Name' or 'Nickname' field if available. May be truncated in source; verify max length against CCH destination schema.

fields.description: Salutation or greeting prefix for correspondence with the contact (e.g., Mr., Mrs., Dr.).
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Salutation' field. Validate values against CCH-allowed salutation list. Null for company-type contacts.

fields.description: Primary street address line for the contact. May contain multi-line address concatenated into a single field.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Address Line 1' (and optionally Line 2 if splitting is applied). PE is UK-origin so address format may differ from US norm — review for line-break delimiters.

fields.description: City or town component of the contact's mailing address.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'City' field. Straightforward; trim whitespace. UK-origin PE instances may store UK city names — validate for US-based acquired firms.

fields.description: County or state component of the contact's address. In UK-origin PE installs this is county; US firms typically store state abbreviation here.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'State' field for US firms. Validate that values are standard 2-letter state abbreviations where CCH requires them. UK county names will require normalization for US-based acquisitions.

fields.description: Postal or ZIP code component of the contact's address.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Zip Code' field. For US firms, validate 5-digit or ZIP+4 format. UK postcodes (e.g., 'SW1A 1AA') indicate a configuration issue for US-based acquired firms.

fields.description: Country component of the contact's address.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Country' field if present. For US-only Ascend acquisitions, most values will be 'US' or 'USA'. Null or blank is acceptable if CCH defaults to US. Normalize inconsistent variants (United States, U.S.A., etc.).

fields.description: Primary business or office phone number for the contact.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Phone' or 'Business Phone' field. Strip non-numeric characters if CCH requires formatted input. Validate 10-digit US format where applicable.

fields.description: Fax number for the contact.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Fax' field. NOTE: A known bug in CCH_Contacts.sql incorrectly mapped ContFamily (a LONG FK) to the fax destination field — ensure this field (ContFax) is correctly mapped to Fax, not ContFamily. Fax data is low priority; null is acceptable if unused.

fields.description: Mobile or cell phone number for the contact.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Mobile' or 'Cell Phone' field. Same formatting rules as ContPhone. High value for partner/principal contacts.

fields.description: Primary email address for the contact. Used for client communications and portal invitations in CCH.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Email' field. Validate format (contains '@'). Duplicates across contacts should be flagged — CCH may reject or warn on duplicate email addresses. Critical field for CCH portal access.

fields.description: Website URL associated with the contact or their business.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Website' or 'URL' field if available. Low migration priority. Validate format if populating.

fields.description: Legacy modem/dial-up number field from early Practice Engine versions. Rarely populated in modern installs.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No corresponding CCH destination field. Do not migrate. Document as unmapped legacy field.

fields.description: Document exchange identifier, a legacy UK-origin delivery address system used in law and accounting firms. Rarely relevant for US firms.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No direct CCH destination equivalent. Likely null for US-based acquired firms. Do not migrate; document as unmapped legacy field.

fields.description: Business name or employer associated with the contact, distinct from ContName when the contact is an individual.
fields.transformation_notes: String. For individual contacts, map to CCH 'Employer' or 'Business Name' field if available. For company-type contacts, may be redundant with ContName. Review CCH Contacts entity for appropriate target field.

fields.description: Office location code or name associated with the contact within the acquiring firm's Practice Engine instance.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH 'Office' field using Ascend's master office code list. Validate all values against Ascend's allowed office codes before load. Mismatches will cause import failure.

fields.description: Self-referencing FK linking this contact to a parent or related contact record within tblcontants. Used to model contact hierarchies or relationships.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK self-reference. Resolve to mapped contact record after all contacts are loaded in CCH. Do not migrate the raw integer; re-resolve after ContIndex remapping.

fields.description: FK referencing the staff member (from tbstaff) responsible for or assigned to this contact.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK to tbstaff. Resolve to CCH staff/user code via staff mapping table. Required for CCH responsible staff assignment on contact records.

fields.description: FK referencing the family group record this contact belongs to. Supports grouping of related contacts (e.g., household or related-entity groupings) in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK. IMPORTANT: A known bug in CCH_Contacts.sql incorrectly mapped this field to the CCH Fax destination field — this is wrong. ContFamily is a grouping FK and has no direct CCH Contacts field equivalent. Do not map to Fax. Assess whether CCH 'Client Group' or 'Household' entity can absorb this relationship.

fields.description: FK linking this contact to its parent client record in tblengagement. The primary join key between contacts and clients.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK to tblengagement (EngIndex or EngClient). Critical join for the CCH Contacts import — used to associate each contact with its CCH client record. Must resolve to migrated CCH client ID before load.

fields.description: Flag indicating whether the contact record has been archived (inactive) in Practice Engine. Typically 0=active, 1=archived.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer flag. Map to CCH 'Status' or filter to exclude archived contacts from migration unless historical data is required. Recommend migrating only active contacts (ContArchive = 0) for wave-one loads.

fields.description: Document folder path or reference associated with the contact in Practice Engine's document management module.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No direct CCH Contacts migration target. Do not migrate. May be relevant for document management integration separately.

fields.description: Date the contact record was originally created in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: Date. Map to CCH 'Date Created' or audit field if available. Retain for historical accuracy. Format as YYYY-MM-DD. Low migration priority if CCH does not accept created-date on import.

fields.description: Date the contact record was last modified in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: Date. Useful for delta-load identification in iterative migration runs. Format as YYYY-MM-DD. Not typically a CCH import field; use for source-side filtering only.

fields.description: Username or staff identifier of the last person to modify this contact record.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Audit trail only. No CCH import target. Do not migrate; retain in staging for traceability.

fields.description: Integer status code representing the active/inactive state of the contact. Works in conjunction with ContArchive.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer. Decode via PE lookup table. Filter WHERE ContStatus = 1 (active) for standard migration scope. Map decoded value to CCH contact status field if available. Confirm lookup values with Tim.

fields.description: Date on which the contact was marked inactive or deactivated in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: String (stored as string despite being a date). Cast to DATE before use. Use to exclude inactive contacts from migration or to populate CCH inactive date field. Filter WHERE ContDateInactive IS NULL for active-only loads.

fields.description: Username or staff identifier of the person who originally created this contact record.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Audit trail only. No CCH import target. Retain in staging for data lineage documentation.

fields.description: Reference ID or path to a profile image associated with the contact in Practice Engine.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No CCH import target for contact images. Do not migrate. Document as unmapped.

fields.description: Free-text notes or comments recorded against the contact record.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Map to CCH Contacts 'Notes' or 'Comments' field. May contain long-form text; verify CCH field length limit. Truncate or append warning if exceeds destination limit.

fields.description: Geographic latitude coordinate stored against the contact's address, likely populated by a geocoding process.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No CCH import target. Do not migrate. Low value field; document as unmapped.

fields.description: Geographic longitude coordinate stored against the contact's address.
fields.transformation_notes: String. No CCH import target. Do not migrate. Low value field; document as unmapped.

fields.description: FK referencing the parent contact record in tblcontants. Joins address records to their owning contact.
fields.transformation_notes: Integer FK to tblcontants.ContIndex. Use as the JOIN key when denormalizing address data into the CCH Contacts import. A contact may have multiple address rows — determine primary address logic (e.g., first row, or Description = 'Main').

fields.description: Full street address for an alternate or additional address associated with a contact. Supplements or overrides ContAddress in tblcontants for multi-address contacts.
fields.transformation_notes: String. When denormalizing, use this field to populate CCH 'Address Line 1/2' for the selected address row. If tblcontants.ContAddress is already populated, evaluate which record represents the primary mailing address using the Description field.

fields.description: Label or type descriptor for this address entry, indicating its purpose (e.g., 'Home', 'Business', 'Mailing', 'Main').
fields.transformation_notes: String. Use to identify the primary or preferred address when a contact has multiple rows in tbladdresses. Recommend selecting WHERE Description IN ('Main', 'Business', 'Mailing') or first row as primary for CCH Contacts address fields.

fields.description: Postal or ZIP code for the alternate address row stored in tbladdresses.
fields.transformation_notes: String. Use alongside tbladdresses.Address when populating CCH 'Zip Code' for the selected address. Same formatting rules as tblcontants.ContPostCode — validate 5-digit US ZIP format. Note tbladdresses does not store city/state separately; may need to parse from Address field or join back to tblcontants for full address construction.
